complaints.narrative:
I am filing a formal complaint regarding {$30000.00} in unauthorized in-person cash withdrawals from my Citibank account on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, at branch locations in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

I did not authorize, initiate, or participate in these transactions. 



1. Transaction Summary XXXX in-person cash withdrawals were processed as follows : * XX/XX/year> : {$25000.00} * XX/XX/year> : {$5000.00} These transactions occurred at Citibank branches in XXXX XXXX and required in-person identity verification. I was not present in XXXX at the time these transactions occurred. 



2. Immediate Response and Account Action On XX/XX/year>, upon discovering the unauthorized withdrawals, I immediately : * Froze my account * Contacted Citibank fraud department * Filed a fraud claim and obtained a case number On the same day, I also filed a police report with XXXX XXXX Sheriffs Office and received an official case number. 



3. Law Enforcement Follow-Up * XX/XX/year> : Initial police report filed in Illinois * XX/XX/year> : After communication with Citibank, I was informed the case should be treated as identity theft and was advised to also file with XXXX  * XX/XX/XXXX onward : I made repeated good-faith attempts to contact XXXX  but was unable to obtain a jurisdiction-specific report number due to inter-agency routing limitations outside of my control * XX/XX/year> : I followed up in person with my local police department, confirmed the case status, and obtained officer contact information for verification purposes 4. Supporting Evidence of Physical Presence I have supporting evidence indicating I was physically present in Illinois during the relevant time period, including : * Employer attendance records confirming work presence on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year> * Workplace records and security/surveillance systems confirming physical presence ( available for verification if required ) * Illinois-based transaction and purchase records on both dates * Timestamped photographs with location metadata confirming my presence in Illinois during the relevant period 5. Bank Investigation and Surveillance Request Given that the transactions were in-person branch cash withdrawals, I requested access to the relevant branch surveillance footage from Citibank locations in XXXX XXXX to assist in verifying the identity of the individual who conducted the withdrawals. 

Citibank has not yet provided the surveillance footage and indicated that the request is subject to internal review and approval and remains pending. 

This footage is directly relevant to confirming the identity of the individual who completed the transactions. 



6. Claim Handling Concerns Despite immediate reporting and full cooperation, Citibank has : * Not issued provisional credit * Continued to delay resolution of the fraud claim * Required a XXXX XXXX police report number despite jurisdictional limitations outside of my control * Not yet provided the requested surveillance footage 7. Request for Resolution I respectfully request that CFPB require Citibank to : * Refund the full {$30000.00} in unauthorized withdrawals * Conduct a full review of branch-level identity verification procedures related to these transactions * Review teller logs, transaction records, and associated surveillance footage * Provide or internally review surveillance footage without further delay * Not delay resolution based on jurisdictional police report requirements that are outside of my control * Provide a written explanation of how these in-person withdrawals were processed Conclusion This case involves unauthorized in-person cash withdrawals with supporting evidence indicating I was not physically present at the transaction locations and did not authorize the withdrawals. 

I acted immediately upon discovery, reported the matter to both the bank and law enforcement, and have fully cooperated throughout the process. Despite this, the claim remains unresolved without provisional credit. 

I respectfully request expedited review and full reimbursement of the disputed funds. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX XXXX Last XXXX digits of account:XXXX

Wells Fargo also did not acknowledge or address the potential negative impact of the credit inquiry on the co-borrower 's credit profile. complaints.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
complaints.company: Army and Air Force Exchange Service
complaints.narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, {$4500.00} was taken from my federal tax refund through the Treasury Offset Program for this alleged debt. 

However, AAFES previously responded to a CFPB complaint stating that this account was charged off on XX/XX/XXXX and marked paid in full on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Despite this paid in full designation, collection activity has continued. When I contacted the collection agency servicing this account, I was informed that multiple Treasury offsets have already been applied over several years, totaling approximately {$15000.00}, in addition to the recent {$4500.00} offset. 

This indicates that nearly {$20000.00} has been collected on this account, yet no complete accounting has been provided, this far exceeds the original balance. 

I have requested a full itemized accounting of the debt, including the original balance, all transactions, payments, offsets, fees, and adjustments. Both AAFES and the collection agency have failed to provide this information clearly. AAFES has refused to discuss the account by phone and directed me to the collection agency, while the collection agency has provided only partial and inconsistent information. 

Additionally, I was not provided with a meaningful opportunity to dispute or review this debt prior to the Treasury Offset. Whenever I try to talk to AAFES, they send me back to the collection agency, who sends me back to AAFES.

Given the prior paid in full status and the significant amount already collected, I am requesting : A complete itemized accounting of the account Clarification of how any balance remained after the paid in full designation Review of whether excessive or improper collections have occurred Refund of any improperly collected funds Suspension of further collection activity pending investigation

complaints.issue: Managing the loan or lease
complaints.narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, a vehicle transaction was processed through a dealership ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and financed through Ally Financial. I was not provided the opportunity to review or approve any retail installment contract or add-on agreements. The only documents shown were limited DMV/title forms. 

During the process, I was sent a one-time PIN and instructed to read it back. I was not informed that this PIN would authorize legally binding agreements, nor did I enter it myself. I was then asked to sign blank signature fields, and I believed I was signing only the visible documents. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, after receiving notice of the loan, I contacted Ally Financial because the financed amount ( {$22000.00} ) was significantly higher than expected, and no contracts had been provided. I later obtained documents showing my name and signature on agreements I never reviewed or authorized, including add-ons totaling {$4300.00} ( service contract and GAP ).

Within one hour of leaving the dealership, the vehicles check engine light activated. By approximately XX/XX/XXXX, the vehicle became inoperable. I incurred costs for diagnostics, towing, and insurance. 

After disputing the transaction on XX/XX/XXXX, and continuing follow-up into early XXXX, the add-ons showed irregular activity. Following media coverage on XX/XX/XXXX, both add-ons were repeatedly canceled, reinstated, and backdated without my authorization : * Service contract ( ~ {$2800.00} ) voided, reactivated ( XX/XX/XXXX ), then canceled again ( XX/XX/XXXX, retroactive date ) * GAP ( ~ {$1500.00} ) canceled ( XX/XX/XXXX ), reinstated ( XX/XX/XXXX ), then canceled again ( XX/XX/XXXX ) On XX/XX/XXXX, Ally Financial credited {$4300.00} after the dealership failed to respond to refund inquiries, indicating the add-ons were not properly validated.

I have paid approximately {$2500.00} toward this loan and incurred additional expenses, with a negative credit impact.

I dispute this loan due to a lack of informed consent, unauthorized use of my electronic signature, and post-transaction manipulation. I am seeking cancellation of the loan, correction of credit reporting, and reimbursement of all losses.

At that time, I observed my computer being remotely controlled without my permission, including my mouse moving on its own. I immediately disconnected the computer. 

Shortly after, I received a text message notification that {$2000.00} had been sent from my bank account via XXXX without my authorization. I did not initiate, approve, or verify this transaction. I reported this immediately to Citibank and filed a fraud claim. My claim and subsequent appeal were both denied based on the transaction being made from a recognized device. However, this does not account for the fact that my device was actively compromised and accessed without my permission. 

I have filed a police report regarding this incident and can provide documentation. This transaction was unauthorized and resulted from account and device compromise. I am requesting a proper investigation and a refund of the {$2000.00}.

complaints.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
complaints.narrative:
XX/XX/XXXX : I made an initial deposit to a Wells Fargo Everyday Checking account. At the time, the amount of money I needed to maintain to avoid a service fee was {$500.00}. I monitored my balance and maintained what I understood to be the required minimum balance in good faith. 

XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX : During this period, I received numerous promotional emails from Wells Fargo for credit cards, CDs, loans, savings accounts, and investment products [ a representative sample can be found in " XXXX XXXX '' ], yet no direct or prominent communication regarding this significant account change. This suggests the bank had the ability to notify customers clearly but did not do so for a change that results in fees. 

XX/XX/XXXX : My XX/XX/XXXX account statement included a notice that, beginning XX/XX/XXXX, the account minimum to avoid fees would increase to {$1500.00}. The notice appeared within the second page of a routine account statement, not in a separate email, letter, app alert, or other prominent notice, despite the fact that the change directly resulted in recurring fees. I believe Wells Fargos method of disclosure was neither adequate nor effective notice for such a significant account change. As a result, I incurred avoidable charges despite maintaining the previously required balance in good faith. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I checked my WF statement, and noticed that service fees had been taken out of the account, totaling {$70.00}. I was confused because I thought I had maintained the minimum balance. I called WF to inquire about this. They said the minimum account balance had been raised to {$1500.00}, without my knowledge. I was refunded {$10.00} of {$70.00} in fees. I escalated the complaint, because I do not believe this is fair. I maintained the previously required {$500.00} minimum balance consistently and reasonably relied on that requirement remaining unchanged absent clear, proactive notice. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I received an email from Wells Fargo ( XXXX ) saying that I needed to " [ Maintain ] a minimum daily balance of {$1500.00} '' to avoid the fees, and that they would not be refunded. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I sent an email ( XXXX ) saying this resolution was not acceptable, and asking that the complaint be escalated. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I received an email from Wells Fargo [ XXXX ] saying the fees would not be refunded, and that " [ Wells Fargo ] recognize [ s ] the feedback that the experience did not feel proactive or clear enough, especially because [ I ] previously maintained the old minimum balance. '' Wells Fargo also stated that it could not approve another refund because my account would not be eligible for another refund of this type for 12 months after the {$10.00} refund issued on XX/XX/XXXX.

complaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint to report Barclays Bank and XXXX XXXX Barclays Bank for failing to properly investigate and resolve a credit card dispute involving a reservation that included a {$1500.00} refundable damage deposit. And XXXX because because they did not return the refundable deposit. 
While I acknowledge that the original transaction was authorized, my dispute is not about the validity of the charge. The dispute is specifically related to the merchants failure to return a clearly designated refundable deposit after the completion of the stay. 
I provided Barclays with documentation confirming : * The total transaction included a {$1500.00} refundable damage deposit * The stay was completed without any reported damages * No claims, invoices, or evidence have been provided by the host or XXXX to justify withholding the deposit * The deposit has not been returned Despite this, Barclays has failed to properly evaluate the dispute. Instead of addressing the non-return of the refundable deposit, they have relied on the fact that the transaction was valid. This is not only irrelevant to the dispute, but it also demonstrates a lack of proper investigation. 
Barclays has not required the merchant to provide evidence supporting their retention of funds, nor have they applied appropriate consumer protection standards under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) or card network dispute rules.

This failure represents : * An inadequate and incomplete investigation * A denial of my rights under federal consumer protection laws * A failure to hold the merchant accountable for contractual obligations I am attaching all relevant documentation, including the receipt and communication attempts. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX

complaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint against Citi, which issues Dillards XXXX Mastercard, for its handling and repeated denial of a billing dispute and its failure to conduct a reasonable investigation.

Citi has denied my dispute multiple times, citing the reason that merchandise was not returned, based on the merchants response. This determination is incorrect because the transaction was for a repair service, not the purchase of merchandise. This misclassification persisted even after I clarified the nature of the transaction and submitted additional evidence.

This charge was for a repair service on my personal device that I had for a year ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ), which involved installing components into my existing phone. There is no standalone merchandise to return. Reversing the service would require disassembling the device and could cause further damage. 

Timeline of Events : * XX/XX/year> : I opened the original dispute. 
* XX/XX/year> : Dispute denied ( merchandise was not returned ). 
* I filed an appeal and clarified that the charge was for a service, not merchandise. 
* XX/XX/year> : Appeal denied for the same reason. 
* On the same day, I contacted customer service and was told no new information had been provided, despite submitting additional evidence showing progressive deterioration of the device after the repair. 
* Later that week, on XX/XX/year>, I contacted Citi again, and a new dispute was opened. The representative documented that the charge was for a phone repair service. 
* XX/XX/year> : Dispute denied again for the same reason. 
* XX/XX/year> : I was informed the case was closed as a duplicate. 
* XX/XX/year> : I spoke with a supervisor who stated the case would be reopened and indicated there was no way to properly classify the dispute within their system.

Evidence Submitted : I provided substantial documentation, including : * Independent diagnostic findings from a XXXX-authorized repair provider confirming the use of non-XXXX  parts and improper repair practices * Photographic evidence showing progressive damage after the repair * A police report related to the device This evidence demonstrates that the service was not performed as described and resulted in further damage.

Additionally, a separate financial institution reviewed substantially similar documentation related to this same merchant and service and issued a refund in my favor. This suggests that the evidence provided is sufficient to support my claim and raises concern that Citi did not meaningfully evaluate the materials submitted.

Nature of Complaint : Citi : * Repeatedly misclassified a service dispute as a merchandise return.

* Failed to meaningfully review or address new evidence * Relied on an inapplicable merchant response * Closed or denied disputes as duplicates despite updated information * Indicated an inability to properly classify the dispute I am concerned this does not reflect a reasonable investigation of a billing error.

complaints.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$1600.00} for a hotel stay with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I stayed at the XXXX XXXX XXXX from XX/XX/year> through XX/XX/year>. During my stay, I experienced multiple serious issues with the room, including unsanitary conditions in the bathtub where debris and dirt came out when the jets were turned on, broken bathtub and shower fixtures, and concerns with the cleanliness of linens. These issues significantly impacted the quality and safety of my stay. 

After returning, I disputed the charge with Wells Fargo, as the services provided were not as described or expected. A provisional credit was issued ; however, it was later reversed while my dispute was still under review. I contacted the claims department and was also contacted by an executive office representative who stated that the matter would be further investigated and that he would follow up. Despite this, the provisional credit was reversed on XX/XX/year>, causing my account to become overdrawn and resulting in additional fees. 

I requested that the provisional credit remain in place while the investigation was ongoing and asked for clarification regarding the status of my dispute. I was given conflicting information by different representatives, and no clear explanation was provided as to why the credit was reversed while the claim was still being reviewed. 

Wells Fargo has since stated that the claim is closed and has refused to provide further assistance, despite the unresolved dispute and the documented issues with the service received. I believe the dispute was not handled properly and that my rights as a consumer were not protected during the investigation process. 

I am seeking a fair resolution, including a partial refund of {$600.00} for the services that were not properly provided, and correction of any fees or negative impacts to my account resulting from the improper reversal of the provisional credit.

complaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding Barclays handling of a billing dispute that I believe was unfairly denied. 

The transaction in question is a charge of {$1600.00} for a resort reservation in XXXX. 

My husband and I were unable to travel due to circumstances outside of our control. We were denied boarding by XXXX separate airlines due to documentation requirements, despite my husband having a valid XXXX residency extension for XXXX more years. As a result, we were physically unable to reach our destination and could not use any portion of the resort stay. 

Importantly : We never checked in at the resort We never used any services associated with this reservation We made multiple good-faith efforts to travel We initially booked a flight with XXXX XXXX  and later rebooked with XXXX XXXX at a significantly higher cost in an effort to still make the trip. We were denied boarding by both airlines for the same reason. I have documentation confirming both bookings, both boarding denials, and refunds issued by both airlines. 

I filed a dispute with Barclays on the basis that the service was not received. However, Barclays denied my claim and sided with the merchant. I was not provided with a clear explanation of how this decision was reached or what evidence was used. 

I believe this decision is unfair because : The service paid for was never rendered I made reasonable and documented efforts to use the service The inability to travel was due to circumstances beyond my control Barclays has failed to properly consider the full context and supporting documentation of my case. 

I also want to note that I made direct efforts to resolve this matter with the resort. Initially, the resort indicated they were willing to offer a partial refund ( approximately 50 % of the total amount paid ). However, this offer was later withdrawn without clear explanation, and I was ultimately denied any refund or credit. 

This inconsistency further demonstrates that the situation was not handled fairly and that there was room for reasonable accommodation, which was ultimately not honored.

complaints.narrative:
XX/XX/XXXX I received 2 fraudulent charges on my XXXX XXXX Citi Advantage card ( I pay and annual fee of $ XXXX {$170.00} for additional user to receive XXXX XXXX XXXX ). I reported them, a new card was issued and the transactions were credited. 

Each day XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX - I received 2 fraudulent charges each day from the same merchant in the XXXX ( I am in XXXX NC and have not been to or purchased anything from the XXXX in a long while ). These charges on a daily basis were ~ {$100.00} from the exact same merchant daily. It is clear that Citi has a security gap in their cards. I suspect the fraudster set these transactions up as recurring charges in a digital wallet. Citi allows those transactions to stay in tact regardless if the card is reported lost or stolen. They put the burden on me as a customer to look for known fraud charges recurring, when i had nothing to do with it to begin with. 

I called on the XXXX to report the fraudulent charges. I expressed frustration that they did not stop the charges. They issued another card ( which I never received ). I called back when I saw the other charges and determined the only way for me to avoid liability and the inconvenience of calling in daily to dispute charges was to close the account. This led to me losing the {$870.00} annual fee that I paid in XXXX of XXXX ( 3 months of benefits received instead of 12 ).

I called to discuss one of two solutions 1 ) partial refund 2 ) reopen the account in a 'blocked ' status so that I can get the benefits due to me ( lounge access, free checked bags, etc ). They indicated that they are unable to refund because the account is closed and that to reopen, they would have to pull credit and 're-approve ' my application.

This is unacceptable. I see charges for 5 straight days not just post, but clear from a known fraudulent location that I have never been and reported as fraud the first day. I then take the only action I can think of to prevent being liable for these ongoing charges- closing the account. I should not lose access to the services I paid for ( XXXX  advantage benefits ) because CITI could not do their job.

complaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding a private student loan associated with XXXX XXXX, as I believe the debt was incurred under misleading and unfair circumstances.

At enrollment, I was not informed of certain academic requirements that were later enforced mid-program, including subjective assignment completion standards that were necessary to progress. While I met all published measurable requirements ( including consistently passing all required knowledge checks above the stated 70 % threshold ), my ability to continue was obstructed by inconsistent grading enforcement and lack of timely instructional support.

I repeatedly requested assistance within required timeframes but experienced significant delays, including response gaps of several weeks. Due to strict course deadlines, this lack of support prevented me from completing assignments as required. Despite passing course assessments, I was later required to revisit and redo prior coursework after progressing, which further hindered my ability to stay on schedule.

As a result of these practices, I was unable to complete the program under the conditions imposed. I attempted to withdraw and seek resolution, but was denied a refund. I am now being held financially responsible for a loan tied to a program I was effectively prevented from completing due to the institutions actions.

I have filed a complaint with the Missouri Attorney General and am seeking relief. I request investigation into the loan obligation and whether it is enforceable given the circumstances, as well as any available options for discharge, cancellation, or other consumer protection remedies.

I can provide documentation including enrollment agreements, grading policies, communication records showing delayed support, and evidence of changing requirements during the program.

complaints.issue: Struggling to pay mortgage
complaints.sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue related to modification, forbearance, short sale, deed-in-lieu, bankruptcy, or foreclosure
complaints.narrative:
Absolutely, XXXX XXXX update your CFPB complaint now that weve confirmed the key point : The XX/XX/XXXX escrow analysis was incorrect because it excluded your XXXX  taxes. 
The XX/XX/XXXX analysis is mathematically correct only because it is fixing that earlier mistake. 
The real issue is that you were issued a surplus check and a lower payment based on a faulty analysis, and then the servicer tried to correct their mistake by calling it your shortage. 
This is exactly the kind of situation the CFPB handles because servicers must follow RESPA rules when they make escrow errors, and they can not penalize the borrower for relying on a written escrow disclosure. 
Below is your updated CFPB complaint, rewritten to reflect the corrected understanding and the actual error. 
________________________________________ Updated CFPB Complaint Servicer Error in Escrow Analysis and Improper Surplus Refund Subject : Incorrect Escrow Analysis, Improper Surplus Refund, and Resulting Shortage After Loan Transfer To the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am filing a complaint regarding my mortgage loan servicer, XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), for issuing an incorrect escrow analysis, sending me a surplus refund check based on that incorrect analysis, lowering my monthly payment, and then reversing the error by creating a large escrow shortage that I am now being asked to repay.

1. XX/XX/XXXX Escrow Analysis Was Incorrect On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an escrow review statement showing a surplus of {$5300.00}. The statement said : YOU HAVE A SURPLUS IN THE AMOUNT OF : {$5300.00} The analysis calculated my new escrow payment as {$750.00}, based on : Property insurance : {$5300.00} Property taxes : {$3700.00} Total : {$9100.00} However, this analysis incorrectly excluded my XXXX  taxes, which were {$3200.00} for XXXX. My loan specialist, XXXX, later confirmed that the XXXX  taxes were not factored into the XXXX analysis. 
Because of this omission, the XX/XX/XXXX escrow analysis understated my true annual escrow obligations and incorrectly showed a large surplus.

2. I Was Issued a Surplus Check Based on the Incorrect Analysis The XXXX statement included a physical check for {$5300.00}, which I was told I could cash or apply to principal or escrow. I relied on this written statement, as required under RESPA.

3. XX/XX/XXXX Escrow Analysis Reversed the Error But Treated It as My Shortage On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a new escrow analysis showing a shortage of {$4600.00} and a new escrow payment of {$1000.00}. 
This analysis included : Property insurance : {$5300.00} Property taxes ( county + XXXX  ) : {$7000.00} Total : {$12000.00} This amount is correct only because it includes the XXXX  taxes that were omitted in XXXX. 
However, instead of acknowledging that the XXXX analysis was incorrect, the servicer treated the correction as a borrower-caused shortage, requiring me to either : Pay {$4600.00} upfront, or Spread it over 12 months, increasing my payment to {$2000.00} 4. The Shortage Exists Only Because the Servicer Made an Error The XX/XX/XXXX analysis is mathematically consistent only because it is correcting the XXXX mistake. 
The shortage is not due to increased taxes or insurance it is due to : The XX/XX/XXXX failure to include XXXX  taxes The issuance of a surplus refund based on that incorrect analysis The lowering of my escrow payment to {$750.00}, which was too low The XX/XX/XXXX correction being applied retroactively as a shortage This means the servicer : 1. Made an escrow calculation error 2. Issued a surplus refund based on that error 3. Lowered my payment based on that error 4. Then attempted to recover the funds by calling it a shortage This is not consistent with RESPA requirements for accurate escrow disclosures. 

I have attached both escrow analyses and my XXXX tax information. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, TX

complaints.narrative:
Product : Credit Card Issue : Billing dispute / Incorrect charge Company : Wells Fargo Bank , N.A . 

Wells Fargo / XXXX XXXX XXXX ending XXXX Disputed Transaction ID XXXX I am filing a complaint regarding Wells Fargos handling of a billing dispute involving an incorrect charge that the merchant has explicitly acknowledged and authorized for a full refund. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a purchase for shoes through XXXX in the expected amount of {$84.00}. However, my Wells Fargo credit card was charged {$120.00} due to a system error on the merchants side. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the merchant sent me a written communication confirming : - The charge amount was incorrect due to a system error - They take full responsibility for the error - They are offering a full refund for the transaction - They are unable to process the refund directly and instructed me to initiate a dispute/chargeback through my payment provider so the full amount could be returned I followed these instructions and filed a dispute with Wells Fargo XXXX Despite providing this documentation, Wells Fargo denied my claim multiple times ( XX/XX/XXXX ; XX/XX/XXXX ; and again after follow-up in XX/XX/XXXX ), stating only that the item was delivered.

This reasoning does not address the actual nature of the dispute. My claim is not based on non-delivery or dissatisfaction with the product. It is a billing error involving an incorrect amount, which the merchant has explicitly admitted and authorized to be refunded.

Wells Fargo has failed to : - Properly consider and evaluate the merchants written admission of error - Address the core issue of an incorrect charge- Conduct a reasonable investigation based on all relevant evidence Instead, they appear to have relied solely on confirmation of delivery, which is not relevant to the billing error dispute.

-- - # # # Desired Resolution : I am requesting that Wells Fargo : 1. Reopen and properly investigate this dispute at a higher level 2. Acknowledge the merchants written admission of error and authorization of a refund 3. Issue a full credit of {$120.00} to my account for the incorrect charge -- - # # # Supporting Documents : - Merchant email dated XX/XX/XXXX confirming system error and authorizing full refund - Original order confirmation showing expected charge of {$84.00} - Credit card statement showing actual charge of {$120.00} - Wells Fargo denial letters dated XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX - Follow-up correspondence with Wells Fargo -- - I have made multiple good-faith attempts to resolve this matter directly with Wells Fargo but have not received a meaningful response addressing the core issue. I am requesting regulatory assistance to ensure a fair and proper resolution. Insurance companies and Big Banks are taking advantage of Consumer 's situation with little recourse to deny and object to even minor claims since they seem well protected and there seems no end to this greed by the companies to enrich themselves on the backs of citizens. Look forward to agencies such as yours to protect ordinary citizens from the predatory hands of these selfish companies who seem to feel that if you deny a claim often enough no one is going to fight and they keep making more profits!!

Thank you.

complaints.narrative:
Iam filing this formal complaint against Citi Bank for their failure to protect my account from a series of unauthorized and fraudulent transactions totaling {$1200.00} USD. 

Facts of the case : Unidentified Merchant : Starting in XX/XX/year> and continuing through XX/XX/year>, my account has been charged multiple times under the merchant name " REVERSE FACEBK '' and similar variations. 

Lack of Authorization : I have never authorized these charges. My debit card is NOT linked to my XXXX account XXXX and it has never been used for any purchases on that platform. 

Evidence of Fraud : My official XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX shows that my only legitimate activity is a subscription billed in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ), which is roughly {$5.00} XXXX. There is absolutely no record of any {$250.00} XXXX  transactions in my XXXX billing history. 

Bank Negligence : I have reported these unauthorized charges to Citi Bank multiple times ( starting in XXXX ). Despite my reports, the bank allowed another {$1200.00} charge to post on XX/XX/year>. 

Violation of Regulation XXXX : The bank claims the merchant " validated '' the charges, but they have failed to provide proof of a signed billing agreement or evidence that these charges originated from my personal account. Under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( Regulation E ), the bank is responsible for refunding unauthorized transactions.

complaints.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
complaints.sub_issue: Credit card company isn't resolving a dispute about a purchase on your statement
complaints.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I was charged for an airline ticket ( XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) booked through Barclays affiliated travel service ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ). 
On XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX cancelled my flight ( XXXX from XXXX to New York ). I did not cancel this trip ; the airline cancelled the service. 
Following the cancellation, XXXX notified me that I may be eligible for a refund and instructed me to contact the travel agency that issued the ticket. I followed these instructions. A refund request was submitted through the travel agency, and XXXX confirmed receipt of the refund request ( Refund Request No. XXXX ). 
Between early XXXX and the dispute filing, I contacted Barclays/XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Representatives repeatedly confirmed that the refund was being processed and that everything was in order. 
However, I never received the refund. 
I then filed a dispute with Barclays. The dispute ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) was denied on the basis that the ticket was non-refundable. 
This reason is not applicable. The flight was cancelled by the airline, and the service was not provided. Under standard airline policy and U.S. Department of Transportation guidance, passengers are entitled to a refund when a carrier cancels a flight and the passenger does not travel, regardless of fare type. 
Despite : The airline cancelling the flight A refund request being properly submitted and acknowledged The service not being provided Barclays denied the dispute and did not issue a credit. 
This reflects a failure to properly investigate and resolve a billing dispute involving services not rendered. 
I am requesting that Barclays reopen the dispute and issue a full refund for the cancelled flight transaction.

complaints.issue: Written notification about debt
complaints.sub_issue: Didn't receive notice of right to dispute
complaints.narrative:
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Complaint Against Wells Fargo I am submitting a complaint regarding Wells Fargos use of right of setoff against funds in my checking account.

Wells Fargo withdrew {$1800.00} from my checking account to apply toward an old Wells Fargo Visa credit card debt. This action completely emptied my account.

The funds taken were court-ordered spousal support arrears that I currently depend on for basic living expenses. I am presently unemployed and rely on these support payments for rent, utilities, food, transportation, and necessities. 

As a result of Wells Fargo removing the funds, my checking account then became negative {$200.00} when smaller automatic payments ( including internet and other essential bills ) posted afterward. 

There is still an ongoing court-ordered spousal support arrears balance of approximately {$20000.00}, being paid at {$3000.00} per month, which demonstrates these are continuing support payments intended for maintenance and support. 
I am not refusing to address the old credit card debt. However, Wells Fargos actions created immediate hardship by seizing support funds and causing my account to become overdrawn.

I am requesting the following relief : 1. Refund all or a substantial portion of the {$1800.00} taken.

2. Reverse the negative balance and any related fees.

3. Cease future setoffs against incoming support payments.

4. Offer a reasonable hardship repayment arrangement for the credit card debt.

5. Provide a written explanation of the legal and contractual basis for taking support-related funds. 

I am requesting prompt review due to financial hardship and unemployment.

complaints.issue: Trouble during payment process
complaints.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding multiple discrepancies, inconsistent information, and errors related to my escrow account and insurance coverage with LoanCare/Lakeview Mortgage XXXX 

On Wednesday, XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXXXXXX XXXX I contacted customer service after repeatedly receiving letters stating that my flood insurance coverage did not meet required limits. Prior to calling, I confirmed with my insurance provider that my flood insurance had already been increased on XX/XX/XXXX, from {$200000.00} to {$220000.00}, which exceeds the required minimum of {$210000.00}. 

During my call, the LoanCare representative acknowledged that their system had not been updated and still reflected the outdated coverage amount. She confirmed this was an internal error and informed me that I would receive a refund of {$280.00} due to the mistake. I also asked if any further action was required on my part, and she assured me that everything was correct and updated. 

However, on Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email stating that a new escrow analysis had been conducted, showing a shortage of {$2600.00}. I was given two options : Pay the full shortage and have a new monthly payment of {$3200.00}, or Spread the shortage over XXXX months, increasing my monthly payment to {$3400.00} This was extremely concerning, especially since I had already undergone an escrow analysis in XX/XX/XXXX, which resulted in a shortage of {$2500.00} and increased my mortgage payment from {$2900.00} to {$3300.00}. My understanding is that escrow analyses are typically conducted annually, so I do not understand why another analysis was performed just four months later. 

Later that evening ( XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX XXXX ), I called again and was told by a different representative that the increase was due to earthquake and fire hazard insurance. This explanation raised concerns, as earthquake insurance is not mandatory in XXXX 

On Friday, XX/XX/XXXX, at XXXXXXXX XXXX I called again and was transferred multiple times. One representative confirmed the earlier flood insurance error and acknowledged the notes in the system. She initially repeated the explanation about earthquake insurance but later corrected herself after reviewing further. She discovered that fire hazard insurance had been duplicated in error, instead of being properly categorized alongside property insurance. The agent stated that she's going to fix the mistake and it was brought to her attention that I was going to receive two refunds, and refund for {$280.00} and {$250.00} that should be issued in XXXX to XXXX business days. According to the last agent I was transferred to told me that because of the whole flood insurance matter that is the reason my escrow has a shortage, I stated that with the two refunds I will be receiving is a total of {$530.00}, and currently my escrow amount is {$1200.00}. The agent stated that my escrow has to be {$4000.00}, I added the {$1200.00} plus the {$530.00} which gave me a total of {$1700.00}, my current standing escrow shortage is {$2600.00}, when I subtract the {$1700.00} from the shortage amount, my grand total was {$860.00}. I told agent how I've been through the Ringer since Wednesday with each representative giving me a different answer. This is the reason why I am writing this complaint

From XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, I made multiple follow-ups via phone and email and was consistently told that the matter was being worked on. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was informed that the funds could not be recovered and was advised to contact the receiving bank directly. I was provided recovery ID XXXX. 

Since that time, I have identified what appears to be the same fraudulent operation continuing under a newly created website, suggesting an ongoing scam using banking channels. 

I am filing this complaint because I believe additional action should have been taken to attempt recovery and to investigate the receiving account involved in this fraudulent transaction. complaints.issue: Unexpected or other fees
complaints.narrative:
Company Name : Comdata / XXXX Product : Payroll Card / Prepaid Card Issue : Deceptive practices, undisclosed fees, and barriers to accessing earned wages Description of Complaint : I am filing a formal complaint against ComdataXXXX for engaging in Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( UDAAP ) and violating provisions of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( Regulation E ) regarding fee disclosures and access to wages. The company utilizes system mechanics and undisclosed fees that systematically trap earned wages and force excessive charges on the consumer. 

The specific violations and deceptive practices are detailed below : XXXX. Predatory ATM Error Generation and Excessive PIN Fees When attempting to access my wages via an in-network XXXX  ATM , the machine rejected my initial, correctly entered PIN. This system error induced a secondary attempt where, second-guessing the initial rejection, an incorrect PIN was entered. The system immediately levied an excessive {$3.00} " Incorrect PIN '' fee. While customer service refunded this initial fee upon request, the sequence indicates a predatory system design : generating a false initial rejection to trigger a user error on the subsequent attempt, thereby generating fee revenue. 

Furthermore, immediately penalizing a single PIN error is inherently abusive given the practical realities of using public ATMs. Consumers are forced to use machines that frequently suffer from poor maintenance, such as sticky or unresponsive keypads. Additionally, these ATMs are located in high-traffic public areas where consumers must maintain situational awareness for their own personal safety. Environmental distractions, or even being physically bumped by other retail patrons, can easily result in a single mis-keyed entry. Punishing a minor, environmentally induced keystroke error with an excessive {$3.00} chargeespecially when the ATM is the primary, forced method to access earned wagesexploits the consumer 's circumstances for profit. I no longer trust the integrity of the ATM network provided by this financial institution. 

XXXX. Artificial Transaction Limits to Prevent Full Withdrawal The approved XXXX ATMs restrict transaction options by failing to provide an interface for custom withdrawal amounts, forcing users to withdraw in preset increments ( e.g., {$20.00} multiples ). Because earned wages are mathematically never paid in perfectly even {$20.00} increments, this design inherently prevents consumers from withdrawing their exact balance. By artificially limiting withdrawals, the system deliberately forces consumers to leave residual balances on the card or execute multiple transactions. This effectively bars the consumer from accessing the entirety of their earned wages for a singular, transparent fee, intentionally trapping residual funds within the XXXX ecosystem. 

XXXX. Undisclosed Third-Party Transfer Fees Upon contacting customer service to seek alternative withdrawal methods due to the unreliable ATM network, the representative explicitly instructed me to use Cash App to transfer my funds. The representative failed to disclose that this method incurs a {$2.00} fee. Directing consumers to third-party platforms to bypass internal system failures without disclosing the associated financial penalties is a clear violation of fee disclosure requirements under Regulation E. 

XXXX. Transfer Delays and Circumvention of Compulsory Use Prohibitions Under 12 CFR 1005.10 ( e ) ( XXXX ) ( Regulation E ), employers and financial institutions are prohibited from requiring employees to receive wages via a specific payroll card. However, this company facilitates a system where the card is issued by default during agency onboarding if personal bank information is not immediately available. Once trapped in this system, the user is penalized for attempting to leave. The initial setup screen advertises a {$1.00} fee for a XXXX day transfer, but this option is inexplicably unavailable when attempting to set up a standard direct deposit to a personal bank. Instead, the direct deposit setup enforces a mandatory XXXX hold. This arbitrary delay traps the consumer 's funds in the ComdataXXXX ecosystem for two full pay periods, forcing reliance on the fee-laden ATM or third-party transfer options outlined above. 
XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX Use Violations Under 12 CFR 1005.10 ( e ) ( 2 ), consumers can not be required to establish an account with a particular financial institution as a condition of receipt of wages. While the onboarding agency failed to provide a viable, immediate alternative to the payroll card ( such as a paper check ), ComdataXXXX system architecture actively facilitates and enforces this " de facto '' compulsory use environment. By imposing an arbitrary and mandatory XXXX hold on the initiation of external direct deposits to an institution designated by the employee, Comdata ensures that newly hired employees are trapped utilizing their proprietary, fee-laden card for a minimum of two pay periods. This systemic friction intentionally eliminates the viability of the consumer 's legal right to an alternative payment method, effectively forcing reliance on the Comdata product and subverting federal consumer protections.
complaints.company_public_response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

complaints.issue: Trouble during payment process
complaints.narrative:
I received an escrow analysis from my mortgage servicer, Lakeview Loan Servicing, servicing handled by XXXX, showing an escrow overage of {$3300.00} that would be refunded if my account was current. 

At the time, my mortgage account was delinquent with a total amount due of {$4100.00}. I contacted the company by phone to request that the escrow overage be applied directly to my outstanding balance so I could bring the account current. 

The company refused and stated that the escrow overage would only be released after the account is brought current. 

This position creates a situation where the servicer is holding a substantial amount of my funds while simultaneously requiring me to independently cure the delinquency, despite those funds being sufficient to cover the majority of the balance due. 

I made a good faith effort to resolve this directly with the company, including speaking with a supervisor, but no resolution or meaningful alternative was provided. 

By refusing to apply funds already held in escrow toward the delinquent balance, the servicer is effectively preventing me from curing the default in a timely manner. 

I am requesting that this matter be reviewed for compliance and exception handling, and that the escrow overage of {$3300.00} be applied to my outstanding balance so I can bring my account current without further delay.

Notice of Lawful Rescission and Perfected Commercial XXXX I am writing to formally notify the CFPB that the automobile purchase agreement ( Account # Description is on the files uploaded to this complaint ) involving CAR TOWN XXXX XXXX XXXX and REGIONAL ACCEPTANCE CORPORATION was lawfully rescinded and voided in its entirety on XX/XX/XXXX. This action was executed pursuant to the Truth in Lending Act ( XXXX ), XXXX XXXX. XXXX, following the discovery of material non-disclosures, fraudulent misrepresentations, and the failure of the " XXXX XXXX '' to provide clear and conspicuous notice of the right to rescind. 
Pursuant to XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( b ), once a consumer rescinds a transaction, any security interest taken in the property becomes void by operation of law, and the consumer is no longer liable for any finance or other charges. Consequently, the continued attempts by Regional Acceptance Corporation to collect on a principal balance of $ XXXX a contract that is legally voidconstitute abusive debt collection practices in violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), XXXX U.S.C XXXX XXXX. 
Furthermore, as the XXXX XXXX XXXX and Holder-in-Due-Course of the private asset identified as XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX the files uploaded to this complaint ), I have perfected a superior international commercial lien via a XXXX Financing Statement. This filing establishes a public record of my first-in-time, first-in-line interest, which is superior to any presumed interest held by the corporate state or the named Lien Debtors. The XXXX is registered at a sum certain of {>= $1,000,000} to cover statutory damages, identity conversion, and fraud. 
The XXXX Debtors were served with a formal Notice of Default and Opportunity to Cure on XX/XX/XXXX, after they failed to return the Manufacturers Certificate of Origin ( XXXX ) and issue a full refund as required by law following the rescission. Their failure to perform constitutes a commercial trespass against my private estate. I request that the CFPB initiate an investigation into these patterns of non-compliance and take administrative action to stop all unlawful collection efforts associated with this voided account.

complaints.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
complaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding unfair overdraft fees caused by delayed funds availability and inadequate customer service. 
I initiated a transfer from my account at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to my Wells Fargo checking account before scheduled payments were due. Based on my prior experience, these transfers have typically been available within a consistent timeframe. 
In this instance, Wells Fargo delayed the availability of my transferred funds. During this delay, transactions were processed and resulted in approximately {$350.00} in overdraft fees. These fees were not due to a lack of funds, but due to Wells Fargo delaying access to funds that had already been transferred. 
I contacted customer service to resolve this issue and request a refund. During one of my calls, I was disconnected by a representative before my issue was resolved. On a subsequent call, I was told that only one overdraft fee could be refunded per year due to internal policy, and only a single fee was refunded.

This response does not address the root cause of the issue, which is delayed funds availability leading to multiple fees from the same incident. Limiting refunds to one per year does not resolve situations where multiple fees are triggered by a single bank action or delay.

I am requesting a full refund of the remaining overdraft fees, as they were caused
by Wells Fargos delayed posting of funds rather than actual insufficient funds at the time I initiated the transfer. 
Additionally, I request a clear explanation of why this transfer was delayed compared to prior transactions and what steps Wells Fargo will take to prevent this from happening again.
complaints.company_public_response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

complaints.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
complaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding an overdraft fee that I believe was unfairly charged due to unclear and misleading instructions from my bank. 

I received an overdraft notice stating that I needed to bring my account current by XX/XX/year>. In response, I deposited a check at approximately XXXX PM on XX/XX/XXXX, before the stated deadline. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the bank released {$270.00} from that deposit. This amount was more than sufficient to cover the overdraft balance on my account. Despite this, the bank still charged me an overdraft fee. 

The notice I received did not clearly explain that only fully available funds ( as opposed to deposited funds ) would count toward bringing the account current. Based on the wording of the notice, I reasonably understood that making a deposit by the deadline would satisfy the requirement. 

I acted in good faith to correct the overdraft within the timeframe provided. The banks subsequent release of funds from my deposit demonstrates that the deposit was valid and sufficient to cover the overdraft amount. 

Because the bank did not clearly disclose that deposited check funds would not count toward bringing the account current, I believe the overdraft fee was assessed unfairly. 

I am requesting that the overdraft fee be refunded and that the bank review its policies and disclosures to ensure they are clear and not misleading to consumers. They did refund only XXXX fee which was XXXX. They are still not willing to refund the whole XXXX that I was charged Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

complaints.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
complaints.issue: Money was not available when promised
complaints.narrative:
Since XX/XX/XXXX, Bank of America has owed me a refund of {$3800.00} due to the cancellation of a vehicle warranty. I have called them several times to request the refund, and : On XX/XX/XXXX, they told me that in XX/XX/XXXX, they had sent me XXXX checks totaling {$3800.00}, but I never received them. I told the representative that I hadn't received the checks yet, and her response was that she would schedule a direct deposit to my checking account, which I would see in approximately XXXX business days. 
Not XXXX business days passed, but 31 days, and I didn't receive the deposit. So, on XX/XX/XXXX, I called Bank of America again to demand the money they owed me. This time, they told me they had sent me checks in XX/XX/XXXX, but those checks haven't arrived either, and it seems I won't be able to get my money back. I've called Bank of America XXXX times and haven't received any response. They always tell me something different ; they acknowledge they owe me a refund, but they don't send my money. I also emailed them demanding the refund, but they haven't responded. I've visited XXXX branches in XXXX, but they tell me I have to resolve the issue by phone. 
Please, I need help resolving this issue. In addition to the money that is rightfully mine, I need Bank of America to compensate me for the delay and inconvenience caused. When I'm late making a payment, the bank charges me high late fees, so the bank should also pay interest when it's late in paying a customer 's money. 

I wrote this letter using XXXX XXXX because I only speak XXXX. If a representative calls me, I will need a XXXX XXXX

complaints.issue: Managing an account
complaints.narrative:
I filed multiple disputes in XXXX related to duplicate transactions and non-delivery of services through an online platform. The issue is straightforward : I paid for services and credits that I ultimately can not access, withdraw, or use. The platform experienced failures including wiped balances, inaccurate tracking of credits, and account lockout. I have provided supporting documentation showing that I had a significant balance that is now inaccessible. 

Despite this, Wells Fargo has taken the position that services were rendered. This conclusion ignores the fact that I do not have access to the account or the ability to use what I paid for. If I can not access or use the services, then they were not delivered. Access and the ability to use or withdraw are fundamental components of the service itself.

The handling of my disputes has been inconsistent, contradictory, and raises serious concerns about compliance with Regulation E. 

The claims were initially opened on XX/XX/XXXX and denied within 24 hours after being routed to the wrong department. This error was later acknowledged by the executive team. The claims were then repeatedly reopened and closed between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, often within very short timeframes and without any clear explanation or documented investigative findings. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received communication stating that more time was needed to complete the investigation. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, the claims were denied. When I contacted the executive team shortly after, the most recent internal notes still stated : * Pending partner response * Verified IMC response should be today * Pending outcome for Regulation E claim These notes confirm that the investigation was still ongoing at the time the claims were denied. Despite this, Wells Fargo proceeded to close the claims. 

In addition, Wells Fargo stated in their response that they are reviewing the other transactions and will provide updates separately. These transactions are part of the same claims that were already denied. This creates a direct contradiction : Wells Fargo can not claim the investigation is complete while simultaneously stating that the same transactions are still under review. This indicates the investigation was not complete at the time of denial. 

There have also been significant inconsistencies in how my claims were handled. The claim from XX/XX/XXXX remains open with provisional credit issued and is still under review. Additionally, I opened another claim on XX/XX/XXXX for a separate transaction, and provisional credit was issued on that claim as well, which also remains open. 

At the same time, the remaining claimsfiled under the same circumstanceswere denied without provisional credit. This demonstrates inconsistent application of Regulation E requirements. Claims where provisional credit is issued remain open and under review, while others are denied despite exceeding the same timeframe and involving similar facts. 

I was also given conflicting information between departments. The claims department advised that provisional credit should have been issued but could not act because the executive team controls the claims. The executive team stated the claims were denied and directed me back to the claims department. This resulted in a continuous runaround with no resolution.

Wells Fargo also failed to follow the requirements of Regulation E under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act. Specifically, under 12 CFR 1005.11 ( c ) ( 2 ), if a financial institution can not complete its investigation within 10 business days, it is required to provisionally credit the consumers account while the investigation continues.

In my case, Wells Fargo explicitly stated that more time was needed to complete the investigation, confirming that the investigation exceeded the 10-business-day timeframe. Despite this, provisional credit was not issued on the majority of my claims.

Wells Fargo can not extend the investigation timeline and deny provisional credit at the same time. The regulation requires provisional credit if the investigation is not completed within the required timeframe.

Additionally, Wells Fargos determination that there are no grounds for a chargeback is inconsistent with established dispute standards. My claims involve non-delivery of services, services not as described, duplicate transactions, and failure to provide access to purchased services. At the time of purchase, there was no clearly disclosed no-refund policy presented through the payment processor.

At this point, the record shows : * Claims were denied while internal notes show the investigation was still pending * Wells Fargo acknowledged the need for additional time but did not issue required provisional credit * Conflicting and contradictory information was provided between departments * Claims were repeatedly opened and closed without clear resolution * Identical claims were handled inconsistently, including some receiving provisional credit while others did not I also want to document a concerning development that occurred while my disputes are still active. 

I have now been notified that my accounts will be closed by XX/XX/XXXX, despite multiple open claims, pending investigations, and formal notices of error and appeals already submitted. At the same time, I have observed a pattern where any new claims I submit are closed within a very short timeframe ( often within an hour ) when provisional credit is not issued. 

This raises serious concerns about how my disputes are being handled. Closing accounts while disputes are active, combined with the immediate closure of newly submitted claims, creates the appearance that my claims are not being fully reviewed or processed under standard procedures. 

Additionally, this pattern is inconsistent with how other claims have been handled, including claims where provisional credit was issued and those claims remain open and under review. The difference in treatment between claims that receive provisional credit and those that do not further highlights the lack of consistency in how my disputes are being processed. 

Given that I have submitted formal notices of error and appeals within the required timeframe, I am requesting that all claims be properly reviewed and handled in accordance with Regulation E requirements, regardless of the status of my accounts.

I have also filed a complaint with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, which has opened a case and is currently reviewing this matter.

I am requesting that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau review this case for compliance with Regulation E and the Electronic Fund Transfer Act. Wells Fargos handling of this matter raises serious concerns about whether proper investigation procedures were followed and whether required consumer protections were applied. 

I also request that these claims be reassigned to a different representatives at a higher level than the current escalation specialist ( XXXX XXXX ). My interactions with the current assigned representative have been inconsistent, with conflicting information provided at different times regarding the status of the claims, provisional credit, and investigation progress. This has contributed to confusion and a lack of confidence in how my case is being handled. 

Given the inconsistencies and communication issues, I am requesting that a different representative review and handle these claims moving forward to ensure an objective, thorough, and compliant investigation.

complaints.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
complaints.narrative: I am disputing this debt because it is not a valid debt that I owe. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was charged {$800.00} and a separate {$49.00}, and I was also placed into a {$3100.00} credit agreement tied to a {$4000.00} modeling portfolio/photography package. At the time of signing, I was shown only selected portions of the documents on a screen and was not given a meaningful opportunity to review the full agreement before signing. When I asked to return later to review the documents and consider my options, that request was denied, and I was told the deal would not be available later. After I later received the full materials, I found written terms that materially contradicted how the transaction had been presented, including language stating that the company was not an agency. On XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed and attempted to cancel the transaction in writing. Despite that, the debt has continued to be treated as valid for collection purposes. I am requesting cancellation of the debt/account, cessation of collection activity, removal or correction of any related credit reporting, and a refund of the {$840.00} already paid.

complaints.sub_product: Home equity loan or line of credit (HELOC)
complaints.issue: Trouble during payment process
complaints.narrative:
In XX/XX/XXXX, NewRez LLC / Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing issued a payoff statement for my HELOC that included all required fees ( {$83.00} ). Based on this payoff quote, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX paid the loan in full so they could assume second lien position. 

After receiving full payoff funds, NewRez/Shellpoint issued me a refund for an overpayment, which included the same {$83.00} fee that had already been quoted and collected. Despite being paid in full, the account was not closed and the lien was not released. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that the lien was still active and preventing my home sale from proceeding. I was then required to pay the {$83.00} fee a second time to initiate closure of the account. The account was subsequently closed effective XX/XX/XXXX, instead of XX/XX/XXXX when the payoff was actually completed.

NewRez/Shellpoint is now treating this as a new payoff and is delaying the lien release by 4060 days. However, their automated system indicates the lien release was already sent to the county on XX/XX/XXXX, with an expected release date of XX/XX/XXXX, and document availability on XX/XX/XXXX. Their representatives have been unable to confirm or explain this discrepancy. 

I have a scheduled home closing on XX/XX/XXXX, which is now at risk due to their failure to properly process a satisfied loan and release the lien in a timely manner.

This is not a new payoff issue. This is a servicing error where the loan was paid in full in XX/XX/XXXX, but the lien was never released.

I am requesting immediate issuance of an executed Satisfaction of Mortgage ( unrecorded copy acceptable ), confirmation of lien release status, and correction of records to reflect the proper payoff date.
complaints.company_public_response: Company believes it acted appropriately as authorized by contract or law

complaints.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
complaints.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
complaints.sub_issue: Credit card company isn't resolving a dispute about a purchase on your statement
complaints.narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I was the victim of a sophisticated impersonation scam involving my accounts at USAA Federal Savings Bank.

I received a phone call appearing to originate from USAAs fraud department, along with a fraud alert text message referencing suspicious activity. The caller claimed my accounts were under active fraud attack and kept me on the phone for approximately four hours.

During the call, unauthorized activity occurred within my account that I did not initiate, including : A mobile deposit of approximately {$10000.00} into my savings account Transfer of those funds from savings to checking Multiple credit card payments ( {$2000.00}, {$2500.00}, {$1500.00} ) A full credit card payoff transaction These actions appeared in my account in real time and demonstrate that a third party had active access to my account during the incident.

During the same time period, the following credit card transactions occurred : {$9300.00} XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) {$4900.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) These transactions were processed as direct credit card charges ( not through my XXXX account ).

After the incident, I reported the fraud to USAA and requested investigations. USAA conducted multiple investigations and acknowledged fraud in several related transactions, issuing credits for those items.

However, USAA denied the above two transactions, classifying them as authorized, and reversed provisional credits. Their most recent denial was issued on XX/XX/XXXX, stating that the information provided was insufficient. 

This determination does not account for the fact that : unauthorized activity was occurring within my account at the same time the transactions were part of a single continuous fraud event the actions were taken under direction of an impersonator with apparent account access Additionally, the merchants associated with these transactions did not provide legitimate goods or services : For the {$9300.00} transaction, I received a shipment containing prop currency ( XXXX money ), not merchandise For the {$4900.00} transaction, I did not receive any goods or services These facts are consistent with known fraud patterns involving fake merchants and fabricated shipment activity. 

Other institutions reviewing this same incident reached different conclusions. XXXX reviewed related transactions and ruled in my favor, issuing refunds totaling approximately {$10000.00}.

Despite this, USAA has continued to treat these two transactions as authorized and has not meaningfully addressed : the evidence of account compromise the broader fraud context or the applicability of chargeback protections for goods not received or misrepresented

complaints.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
complaints.issue: Problem when making payments
complaints.narrative:
I have a Citibank credit card and for 30 years, we have agreed that Citibank automatically debit my monthly balance from my bank, so in my 30 years I have never held a balance. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I was surprised to incur a {$21.00} interest charge due to a balance from my XXXX statement. Citibank, instead of withdrawing {$1400.00} to pay off my XXXX balance, instead withdrew unexplainably only {$1000.00}. Citibank refunded the {$21.00} and promised to look into the automatic withdrawal system so that I would not have a balance. I did not hear from anyone as to what happened or how it would not happen again. I filed a complaint with CFPB and did not hear anything. 

I was mortified to see that this continued on to this month. On XX/XX/XXXX Citibank charged me {$65.00} in interest even though Citibank correctly withdrew {$2800.00} in order to pay the balance from the XXXX statement and all charges from the XXXX statement. My account was paid off fully for the XXXX statement, yet Citibank somehow found cause to once again charge me interest. When I asked senior manager XXXX why this was, she apologized and offered to refund the money, but could not explain what happened, only that interest is charged to the average daily balance which for the month of XXXX contained both a XXXX balance in addition to my charges from XXXX. 

I appreciate the refund of interest charges, however I can not trust a bank who repeatedly has billing errors, and who can not fix these errors or tell me how they are going to remedy this for future billings. 

Their repeated action to fail to withdraw the full amount per our agreement is illegal against the Fair Credit Billing Act since creditors must credit the account on the day received and in the manner we have agreed upon, and by withdrawing an amount insufficient to cover the balance and thereby incur interest charges is a violation of this FCBA and our cardholder agreement.

This " double cycle billing '' is also illegal under the Credit Card Act of 2009, which requires fair and transparent billing and interest calculation.
complaints.company_public_response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

complaints.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
complaints.issue: Fees or interest
complaints.narrative:
I am writing to file a formal complaint against Citibank , N.A . regarding what I believe to be an unfair and deceptive balance transfer offer made in connection with my Citi / AAdvantage Platinum Select Card ( account ending in XXXX ). Despite initiating a balance transfer under my understanding that it carried a 0 % promotional annual percentage rate ( APR ), I was instead enrolled in a 0.99 % APR offer without clear, unambiguous disclosure at the point of acceptance. I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this matter and assist in reaching a fair resolution. 

I. Account and Transaction Details The following account and transaction information is provided for reference : Cardholder : XXXX XXXX XXXX Account Number ( last 4 ) : XXXX Card Product : Citi / AAdvantage Platinum Select Card Balance Transfer Date : XX/XX/XXXX Balance Transfer Amount : {$15000.00} Balance Transfer Fee Charged : {$750.00} ( 5 % of transfer amount ) APR Applied ( per statement ) : 0.99 % ( BalTrnOffer 6, expiring XX/XX/XXXX ) Interest Charged First Billing Period : {$9.00} Statement Billing Period : XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX XXXX. Description of the Complaint When I accepted the balance transfer offer from Citibank, my understanding was that the promotional rate was 0 % APR. Citibank simultaneously offered both a 0 % APR balance transfer and a 0.99 % APR balance transfer to me as a customer. The presentation of two nearly identical offersone at 0 % and one at 0.99 % is, in my view, a confusing and potentially deceptive practice designed to obscure the material difference between the offers and increase the likelihood that a consumer would inadvertently accept the interest-bearing option. 

Upon receiving my first statement for the billing period ending XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that the balance transfer had been applied at 0.99 % APR rather than 0 %, resulting in a {$9.00} interest charge and a {$750.00} balance transfer fee on a {$15000.00} transfer. Had I known the rate was 0.99 % and not 0 %, I would not have accepted this offer under these terms. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Citibank customer service and spoke with a supervisor. I explained that I believed the offer I had accepted was presented in a misleading manner, and I raised the concern that offering a 0 % and a 0.99 % balance transfer simultaneously without sufficiently clear differentiations inherently confusing and unfair to consumers. The supervisor ensure me that this would be corrected, and the interest refunded, a promise it would be taken care of. 


On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank sent a written response to my inquiry. The letter stated the following : This letter is in response to an inquiry concerning your Citi / AAdvantage Platinum Select World Elite MasterCard account with us. Unfortunately, we are unable to change the Balance Transfer offer once it has been processed. 

This response is inadequate and dismissive. Citibank provided no explanation of why both a 0 % and a 0.99 % offer were simultaneously extended to me, nor any justification for why the consumer bears the entire burden of their unclear offer presentation. It is for this reason that I am escalating this matter to the CFPB. I believe this matter involves potential violations of the Consumer Financial Protection Acts prohibition on unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices ( UDAAP ), as well as potential Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) / Regulation Z disclosure requirements governing the clear and conspicuous presentation of credit card terms. A practice that offers a consumer two similar promotional rates simultaneously, has the reasonable tendency to mislead a consumer and cause material financial harm.
complaints.company_public_response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

Subject : Failure to Reconcile Assets/ Violation of Fair Credit Billing Act Merchant : XXXX ( XXXX # XXXX ) Disputed Amount : {$850.00} Description of Issue : I am filing this complaint because Wells Fargo ( WF ) has failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into a billing error, incorrectly classifying a separate Asset Sale as a Merchandise Refund.

Contractual Pricing Error : Per Attachment 1 ( Invoice ), the agreed-upon net price was {$230.00}. However, the merchant charged my account the full retail price of {$850.00} ( Attachment XXXX ). 

Asset Sale vs. Refund : Wells Fargo denied dispute # XXXX claiming I had been " refunded ''. This is factually incorrect. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX : Attachment XXXX ( Email ) explicitly confirms that the {$620.00} in credits for Order XXXX are " Trade in Credits '' for my separate XXXX XXXX Plus asset. 

Bank XXXX Evidence : Attachment XXXX ( XXXX ) shows XXXX specific credits ( {$350.00}, {$140.00}, {$87.00}, {$36.00} ) totaling {$620.00}. These credits match the merchants trade-in confirmation exactly and are not a refund for the XXXX hardware. 

The Failure to Reconcile : By denying the dispute, Wells Fargo is allowing the merchant to keep my {$850.00} cash while misidentifying the payment for my trade-in asset as a refund. The merchant has further blocked a hardware return through an internal XXXX processing delay.

complaints.issue: Repossession
complaints.sub_issue: Lender trying to repossess or disable the vehicle
complaints.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding serious issues with the handling of my husband 's auto loan account, including payment mismanagement, threat of repossession, and failure to return funds. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was granted possession of my vehicle through a TPO. Following this, my daughter, XXXX, made payments on my behalf to ensure the account remained in good standing. XXXX payments of {$510.00} each were sentone in XXXX and XXXX in XXXX. 

The handling of these payments has been inconsistent and has directly led to financial harm and risk of repossession : The XXXX payment was not accepted and was returned. 
The XXXX payment was accepted and cashed, as confirmed by records. 
After cashing the XXXX payment, the company attempted to return the funds to XXXX. 
XXXX rejected the returned check, and as a result, the {$510.00} has not been returned to me. 
I am now missing {$510.00} that was taken from my daughter 's account and never properly refunded. 

In addition, when I initially contacted the company in XXXX, I attempted to resolve the situation and provide documentation of the XXXX. At that time, the company refused to communicate with me or review my documentation. 

Despite making payments in good faith and attempting to resolve the issue early, I have been : Threatened with repossession Denied access to my daughter 's funds after they sent it back to her bank. Her bank wouldn't accept it. 
Given inconsistent and unhelpful information about my account Only after a complaint was filed did the company begin communicating with me. 

This situation is unacceptable. I made every effort to comply and prevent escalation, but due to the companys actions, I am now facing repossession and financial loss.

Desired Resolution : Immediate halt to any repossession activity, as payments were made in good faith and errors were caused by the companys payment handling.

Immediate return of the {$510.00} that was cashed but never properly refunded.

A full and accurate accounting of my loan, including how payments were applied. 
Written confirmation that my account will be reviewed in light of the TPO dated XX/XX/XXXX and the payments made. 

I am requesting prompt resolution of this matter.
