complaints.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
complaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint against POSSIBLE Fabco located at for multiple willful violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), XXXX XXXX. XXXX et seq., and the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), XXXX XXXX. XXXX et seq. 

This company is currently reporting an alleged debt of {$1500.00} originally owed to my consumer credit reports with XX/XX/orTransUnion. This account is inaccurate, unverified, and/or past the applicable statute of limitations. 

On XXXX/XXXXXX/XX/3026I sent a written Cease and Desist and Debt Validation letter demanding that this company : ( XXXX ) cease all collection activity ; ( XXXX ) provide full written validation of the alleged debt; and ( XXXX ) immediately remove this account from my consumer credit reports. As of today, the company has [ FAILED TO RESPOND/ FAILED TO VALIDATE/ CONTINUED REPORTING / CONTINUED CALLING. 

VIOLATION XXXX REPORTING INACCURATE AND UNVERIFIED DEBT ( FCRA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : This company is reporting an account on my consumer credit file that has not been properly verified. Despite receiving my written dispute, this company has continued to report this unverified account to all three credit bureaus in direct violation of FCRA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( b ), which requires a reasonable investigation and correction or deletion of any information that can not be verified. 

VIOLATION XXXX REPORTING TIME-BARRED / OBSOLETE DEBT ( FCRA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( a ) ( XXXX ) ) : The alleged debt referenced above is past the applicable statute of limitations and/or the XXXX-year credit reporting period established under federal law. The date of first delinquency is UNKNOWN NOT PROVIDED ]. Continuing to report and collect on a time-barred debt without proper disclosure constitutes a deceptive practice in violation of FDCPA XXXX XXXX. XXXX. 

VIOLATION XXXX FAILURE TO VALIDATE DEBT ( FDCPA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : This company failed to provide legally sufficient validation of this alleged debt. A collection notice, computerized record, or basic account summary does not meet the legal standard for validation. The company has not produced the original signed agreement, a complete payment history, or proof of legal authority to collect in my state. 

VIOLATION XXXX CONTINUED COLLECTION ACTIVITY AFTER WRITTEN DISPUTE ( FDCPA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( c ) ) : After receiving my written Cease and Desist, this company continued reporting, calling, sending letters, etc. ], in direct violation of FDCPA XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( c ), which requires a debt collector to cease communication upon written request. 

This unlawful collection and reporting activity has caused me significant harm including : credit score damage, denial of loan/housing/job, higher interest rates, emotional distress, financial hardship ]. 

I am demanding that the CFPB investigate this matter, require this company to immediately delete this account from all three credit bureaus, and take appropriate enforcement action. I have attached my Cease and Desist letter, certified mail tracking confirmation, and supporting documentation. All records have been preserved for potential civil litigation. 


I am requesting : ( XXXX ) Immediate deletion of this account from Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion ; ( XXXX ) Written confirmation of deletion sent to my mailing address within 4 days ; ( XXXX ) Written confirmation that all collection activity has permanently ceased. 

( XXXX ) A refund of any amounts collected in violation of the FDCPA or FCRA ( if applicable ) ; ( XXXX ) Formal enforcement action Fabco for willful violations of federal law.

complaints.narrative:
I am writing to formally request a review and correction regarding a Form XXXX issued in my name for theXX/XX/2023 tax year. 

According to the document provided, a corrected Form XXXX was XXXX by Nonprofits Insurance XXXX XXXX California XXXX {$7500.00} as Other Income under Box XXXX, with no federal income tax withheld. 

I am disputing the accuracy and classification of this reported income. This amount does not accurately reflect taxable income in a manner that has been properly explained, validated, or supported. Additionally, I was not provided with sufficient documentation at the time of issuance to justify how this amount was calculated or why it was categorized as miscellaneous income. 

This discrepancy has caused significant financial hardship and has directly impacted my ability to properly file my taxes and receive any refund I may be entitled to. Reporting income without withholding, particularly when it is unclear or potentially misclassified, places an unfair burden on the recipient and raises serious concerns regarding compliance and reporting accuracy. 

I am formally requesting the following : A full explanation and breakdown of how the {$7500.00} amount was determined. 

Verification that this income was correctly classified under IRS guidelines.

Immediate correction or reissuance of the form if any errors are identified.

Assistance in ensuring that any resulting tax liability is corrected and that I receive any refund owed to me. 

If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will have no choice but to escalate this issue through the appropriate regulatory agencies, including the Internal Revenue Service and other governing bodies, for further investigation. 

Please treat this matter with urgency, as it directly affects my financial stability and tax obligations.

complaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding XXXX XXXX denial of my fraud claim for three unauthorized credit card charges totaling {$350.00} from an online automotive parts retailer.

All three transactions occurred and were posted on XX/XX/year>, according to Wells Fargos own records in their mobile app. 

I discovered these charges the morning of XX/XX/year>, and immediately contacted Wells Fargo at approximately XXXX XXXX  to report the fraud and cancel my card before leaving for work. 

I reported these charges as fraudulent because I did not make or authorize these purchases, did not receive any goods, and no one in my household authorized the transactions. Despite my prompt reporting, Wells Fargo denied my claim, stating that the transactions were associated with an IP address. 

An IP address alone does not constitute proof of authorization. IP addresses can be shared, reassigned, or masked through VPNs and do not reliably identify a specific individual. Wells Fargo has not provided any evidence demonstrating that I personally authorized these transactions. 

Specifically, Wells Fargo has failed to provide : - Proof of delivery to my address - A signed receipt or evidence of card-present use - Verified device or account login data clearly tied to me- Any documentation showing I benefited from these purchases I am requesting that Wells Fargo provide all documentation and evidence relied upon in their investigation, including IP data, timestamps, device information, and any merchant verification details.

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, I am not liable for unauthorized charges. Wells Fargos reliance on insufficient technical data, without proof of authorization, does not meet the standard required to hold me responsible.

I am requesting : 1. Immediate reversal of the {$350.00} in fraudulent charges 2. Reimbursement of any associated fees or interest 3. A full explanation and documentation of the investigation conducted If this matter is not resolved promptly, I am prepared to pursue further remedies, including filing a claim in small claims court.

Desired Resolution : Refund Amount in dispute : {$350.00}

Due to the Citi card declined notice my Fiancs ordered the exact same order with his credit card associated with his address totaling {$320.00}. XXXX  confirmation order # XXXX.

I also contacted XXXX to cancel order # XXXX that I thought was declined on XX/XX/XXXX. XXXX advised I would receive a full credit but only receive {$22.00} of the {$320.00}. CitiCard statement attached supports the {$22.00} credit and the dispute. 

XX/XX/XXXX I contacted XXXX again about my refund. The representative stated an additional credit of {$68.00} could not be processed because the dispute was blocking the request. However, once the dispute was lifted the credit would be applied. Also, once the items were returned additional credits would follow. 

XX/XX/XXXX I received the returned labels and returned the remaining items on XX/XX/XXXX. Credit of {$210.00} would be applied. I was also charged {$7.00} process fee from total {$320.00}. If the credit were applied timely it would have satisfied the disputed balance.

complaints.narrative:
I am submitting a complaint regarding excessive and repeated overdraft ( NSF ) fees assessed on my account within a very short timeframe, which I believe to be unfair and disproportionate. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was charged an overdraft fee of {$72.00}. On XX/XX/year>, I was charged an additional overdraft fee of {$36.00}. In addition, I currently have a pending overdraft fee of {$100.00}, bringing the total overdraft-related fees to {$210.00} within approximately a XXXX hour period. 

These fees appear to have been triggered by multiple small transactions, including : * XXXX XXXX {$23.00} ( XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX {$5.00}, {$3.00}, and {$7.00} ( XX/XX/year> ) * XXXX {$7.00} and {$29.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$58.00} * Additional pending transactions including XXXX {$19.00} and XXXX {$80.00} The total amount of the transactions is significantly lower than the total overdraft fees assessed. The bank charged multiple fees in rapid succession rather than limiting or declining transactions once the account became overdrawn. 

Actions taken : I reviewed my account transactions and identified that multiple overdraft fees were charged for transactions processed closely together. These charges compounded quickly, creating a negative balance that continued to trigger additional fees. 

Issue : The overdraft fee practices appear excessive and punitive, especially given : * Multiple fees charged within a single day or short period * Fees exceeding the amounts of individual transactions * Lack of reasonable limits or safeguards to prevent repeated charges Requested resolution : I am requesting : XXXX. A full review of the overdraft fees assessed on April 2021, 2026 XXXX. A refund or reduction of the excessive overdraft fees XXXX. Clarification of the banks overdraft fee policy, including how multiple fees are triggered within such a short timeframe XXXX. Implementation of reasonable limits to prevent excessive fee stacking in the future

complaints.issue: Closing on a mortgage
complaints.sub_issue: Closing disclosure or other related disclosures
complaints.narrative:
Overview The lender, Lennar Mortgage, has engaged in a pattern of deceptive practices, coercion, and repeated failures to provide accurate federal disclosures ( TRID ) during the closing process for a DSCR mortgage. After being promised a {$6000.00} seller credit for purchasing a XXXX XXXX, we learned that we are required to use Lennar Mortgage and XXXX XXXX in order to obtain the credit. However, Lennar padded the closing costs with fees that went back to the seller 's affiliated companies. We were willing to negotiate terms. However, despite multiple notifications of errors, the lender has consistently provided documents with incorrect financial figures and used high-pressure tactics to force signatures on inaccurate disclosures. They have promised accurate paperwork multiple times and missed promised timelines. When sent reminders, they have responded saying they will get it within XX/XX/XXXXdays, or XXXX hours, even though those timelines are already passed. 

Primary Lender Representative : XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ID : XXXX ) Chronological Events & Actions Taken XX/XX/year> : When asking clarifying questions, our representative stated repeatedly that he did not know the answers. He made inaccurate statements saying that we already agreed to terms we had never discussed. The company representative also repeatedly sent emails demanding signature, and threatened a $ XXXXper-day penalty fee ( per contract ) for " holding up the process. '' The lender agreed to revised terms for the loan, including APR and discount points, but provided a Closing Disclosure ( CD ) containing old figures. When I asked for the corrected disclosures before signing, he stated that I had to sign or would risk losing the loan. He promised new closing disclosures within XXXX business days. 

XX/XX/year> : I sent an email again requesting the revised closing disclosures, and was told I would get them in XXXX business days. This was the XXXX business day. 

XX/XX/year> : I sent an email again requesting the revised closing disclosures, and was told they were not ready. 

XX/XX/year> : At XXXX am, the lender provided a Closing Disclosure ( CD ) containing significant errors in the seller credit and other closing costs. We then received an email and form from XXXX XXXX stating our final cash to close amount and disclosures, relying on the inaccurate figures, and stated that we must wire the money by XXXX business days before closing ( which would be XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, or XX/XX/XXXX ). The agreed-upon credit was {$6000.00}, but the lender listed it as {$2400.00}. It listed Hazard insurance for {$1300.00} due at closing, even though we had already provided receipts for paid in full on XX/XX/XXXX. Furthermore, the lender incorrectly listed a {$1300.00} hazard insurance premium as " Paid at Closing, '' despite having received proof of payment ( " Paid Before Closing '' ) on XX/XX/XXXX. It was missing credits for seller-paid prorated figures. I formally requested a correction via email, phone, and SMS with both several company representatives. The loan officer has been unresponsive to multiple emails from both the borrowers and the title company, often replying outside of business hours or with " out of office '' notifications. The only response I received was a phone call with XXXX XXXX, telling us to contact Lennar Mortgage representative, as they were responsible for providing accurate figures. 

XX/XX/year> : The representative acknowledged the error, but insisted I sign the incorrect documents to meet a deadline, promising a fix later. The lender sent additional documents that still contained the incorrect closing figures and cash to close. We finally received a response from Lennar Mortgage. 

XX/XX/year> : We again emailed with our questions, and received a promise of a response and final cash-to-close at least XXXX before closing, even though that deadline had already passed. A new set of documents was provided late afternoon, which for the first time, included a vague, nondescript Prepayment Penalty with no specific explanation and Lack of Escrow. This significant financial term was never previously disclosed and contradicts prior assurances that the loan could be paid off early. When we asked for clarification, XXXX falsely claimed we already knew and agreed to these. The new documents still contained errors that affected cash to close. We were urged to wire cash-to-close without knowing the accurate amount required for our wire transfer, with the promise of a refund. 

XX/XX/year> : As of XXXX hours before the scheduled closing on XX/XX/XXXX, the lender has still not provided an accurate, balanced Closing Disclosure. We have received more documents to sign today that still contain the inaccurate figures, and another inaccurate cash-to-close number. We received for the first time an escrow waiver and an incomplete XXXXpage document in the portal that states The Cash to Close amount referenced in the Costs at Closing section on page XXXX and the Calculating Cash to Close section on page XXXX represent cash to close associated with fees and costs incurred for this loan transaction. We have not received answers to our questions or a finalized and corrected cash-to-close statement. From XXXX, we received a final XXXX transaction document with incorrect figures still in place. 

Summary of Complaints : Coercion : The lender sent numerous bold and underlined emails, and made verbal statements attempted to force the signing of legally binding financial documents known to be inaccurate through the threat of daily financial penalties or loss of sale. 

XXXX Violations ( XXXX ) : The lender has repeatedly failed to provide a corrected Closing XXXX within the federally mandated three-business-day window prior to closing. The lender sent us waivers to sign only after we pointed out the inaccuracies. 

Deceptive XXXX Practices : Promising seller credits, but only if the consumer agrees to use their affiliated companies. The late-stage introduction of a prepayment penalty and the consistent inaccuracies regarding closing costs suggest deceptive lending practices. 

False statements : The lender representative made several false statements in response to our questions or requests regarding significant matters, claiming that we had already agreed to such terms, including the XXXX and XXXX XXXX. 

Refusal to Answer Questions : Questions from myself, my husband, and my realtor were repeatedly dismissed or left unanswered. We were ignored, yelled at and presented with false statements when seeking clarification of changing numbers, missed deadlines, or inaccurate documents.

complaints.narrative:
Issue : Failure to process XXXX interest rate reduction on credit card and inability to access account due to verification barriers I am an XXXX XXXX servicemember who submitted valid military orders to USAA Federal Bank on XX/XX/year> requesting application of protections under the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act ( SCRA ), specifically the 6 % interest rate cap on my credit card account. 

As of today, it has been over 30 days since I submitted my orders. Despite this, USAA has not processed my request, has not provided a status update, and continues to charge interest above the 6 % XXXX cap. 

I attempted to resolve this through the banks mobile app chat. I was instructed that I must call customer service. I then called the bank ; however, I was unable to complete account verification because the bank requires a one-time passcode to be sent to my phone number. I am currently XXXX in a location where my cellular service does not function, and I am only able to communicate over XXXX. As a result, I am unable to receive these verification codes and can not access support to resolve this issue. 

This creates an unreasonable barrier preventing me from exercising my rights under XXXX. 

Under XXXX, creditors are required to reduce interest rates on pre-service obligations to 6 % upon receipt of valid orders, and this reduction must be applied retroactively to the start date of XXXX XXXX service. Any interest charged above 6 % must be forgiven. 

I have made a good faith effort to provide all required documentation and to follow the banks procedures. The continued delay and inability to access assistance due to verification limitations is unreasonable.

I am requesting the following : 1. Immediate application of the 6 % SCRA interest rate cap to my credit card account XXXX. Retroactive adjustment of the interest rate to my XXXX XXXX start date : XX/XX/year> XXXX. Refund or credit of any excess interest charged above 6 % since that date XXXX. A reasonable alternative method of identity verification that does not require cellular service XXXX. Written confirmation that SCRA protections have been properly applied If additional documentation is required, I request that the bank clearly communicate what is needed without further delay. 

This issue has been pending for over 30 days without resolution, and I am seeking prompt corrective action.

complaints.narrative:
Complaint against Citibank , N.A . Failure to Conduct Reasonable Investigation ( Dispute ID : XXXX ) [ INTRODUCTION THE IMPACT ] I am filing this formal complaint because the bank has failed to fulfill its legal obligations under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). I purchased furniture for my XXXX sister, who suffers from XXXX XXXX. I was physically present at the delivery location ( XXXX XXXX ) specifically to receive and assemble the item for her on XX/XX/year>. The item never arrived. This ordeal has caused significant and unnecessary distress for my elderly sister and myself. 

[ EYEWITNESS ACCOUNT & IMMEDIATE ACTION ] On the day of delivery, I was waiting inside the apartment. The moment I received the delivery notification on my phone, I immediately went outside to the hallway and entrance. The furniture was not there. There was no one in the hallway, and no knock had been made. I called the merchant immediately to report that the item was missing despite the " delivered '' status. Please see the attached " Communication and Evidence Log '' PDF, which documents my immediate attempts to resolve this and the photographic proof I gathered at the scene showing the discrepancy between the correct building and the misdelivered building. 

[ THE SPECIFIC ERROR : WRONG BUILDING, SAME UNIT ] The dispute for {$210.00} stems from a clear driver error. While the destination was XXXX XXXX in the correct building, the merchant 's delivery photo matches XXXX XXXX at a neighboring building. I provided the bank with side-by-side photographic evidence proving the location in the delivery photo is the wrong building : Permanent Door Features : The door at the correct address has a XXXX vertical stainless-steel plate near the handle ; the door in the delivery photo is missing this feature. 

XXXX XXXX : The door hardware at the correct address is on the opposite side of what is shown in the misdelivered photo. 

Surrounding Amenities : The delivery photo shows a bench next to the door that exists at the neighboring building but does not exist at the correct delivery building. 

[ PROCEDURAL FAILURE BY THE BANK ] I made XXXX good-faith attempts to resolve this with the merchant. A supervisor admitted the misdelivery on a recorded line and promised a refund within XXXX hours, which never arrived. 

The banks investigation was not " reasonable '' as required by the XXXX. I submitted the photographic proof and detailed explanation on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>. Instead of reviewing this material evidence, the bank issued a final denial on XX/XX/year>, claiming the " timeframe has passed. '' A bank can not use a technicality to ignore proof that a merchant 's claim is factually incorrect. Misdelivery to the wrong building is legally a " non-delivery. ''

complaints.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
complaints.sub_issue: Credit card company isn't resolving a dispute about a purchase on your statement
complaints.narrative:
I am writing to formally appeal Citibanks recent determination regarding my dispute with Wayfair. I was advised that the dispute was denied because it was determined that the merchants cancellation and return policy was not followed. This conclusion is incorrect and appears to overlook the documentation and timeline I previously provided.

I followed the merchants return policy completely and within the allowed timeframe. 
On XX/XX/year>, I initiated a return request online as required by Wayfairs instructions for three ceiling fans I purchased. The items were not to my satisfaction upon arrival. The return was initiated well within the standard 30-day return window, and in fact the holiday return period had been extended through XX/XX/year>. 
Wayfair itself acknowledged my return request. I received confirmation on XX/XX/year>, at XXXXXXXX  stating that the return had been received and I would shortly receive a follow-up email confirming the pick-up date for the items. 
However, more than three months has now passed and that promised email was never sent, essentially denying me the ability to return my purchase as was my right. 
Wayfair requires that these items be returned only through a pick-up arranged by their designated carrier, XXXX. I attempted to contact this company myself using the telephone number provided by Wayfair and discovered the number had been disconnected. It appears the company is no longer operating. 
I immediately notified Wayfair and provided proof of this issue. Despite doing so, Wayfair has never contacted me to arrange a pick-up or provide an alternative method for returning the items. 
I made multiple good faith attempts to complete the return process : XXXX initiated online per Wayfair instructions. 
XXXX Wayfair customer service. I was told I could return the items via XXXX  XXXX. 
XXXX went to a XXXX  XXXX location and attempted to return the items, only to be told this was not permitted because they are large items. 
XXXX I contacted Wayfair again and was told the prior information was incorrect and that only XXXX  could pick up the items from my home. I advised the representative the phone number for XXXX was disconnected. 
Wayfair advised there was nothing further I could do and provided no alternative solution. 
As a consumer, I can not force a merchants logistics provider to pick up merchandise. I followed every step required by the merchants return policy and went above and beyond to attempt to return the items. The failure to complete the return rests entirely with the merchant and their inability to coordinate pick-up. 
The items remain available for return and have been ready for pick-up since the return was initiated. 
I also previously informed Wayfair that I would dispute the charge if the return could not be completed because I did not want to lose my right to a refund due to delays on the merchants side. 
I am extremely disappointed that Citibank concluded I failed to follow the merchants return policy when the documentation clearly demonstrates that I did follow it completely. 
It is irresponsible and frustrating that not only did Citi not investigate this matter properly, but they also sent me a threatening letter advising they would report me to XXXX, XXXX  and XXXX  for failure to pay. These threatening tactics and language were seemingly done to intimidate me, however, I am still hopeful the " system '' in place to protect me will function properly. Citi encouraged me to 'seek other means to resolve this matter '', so here I am. 
I am requesting a full refund credit in the amount of {$950.00} for the returned merchandise that the merchant has failed to retrieve despite my compliance with their return policy.
